Avoma call 13 May 2026: Mark Patton stated 'we do not have a good system in place to provide feedback on getting statuses on these items... we struggle with getting updates on it... that creates... potential line stopping because the lack of inventory for those critical items'

metrics.narrative: Pain is confirmed and operationally specific, but not yet quantified in dollar or time-savings terms. Francisco Güereca stated on 13 May 2026 that over 95% of purchase requisitions are manually converted from PR to PO in SAP, creating inefficiency and delay. Mark Patton described on 13 May 2026 the operational pain of zero real-time tracking on critical items: when critical inventory hits zero stock, the procurement team struggles to provide delivery updates, risking production line stoppages. Francisco noted on 13 May 2026 that suppliers are chronically late with no contractual compensation clauses, and that Axis Pipe runs manual reports to estimate lead times because the system does not track them. The pain owner is clear: Francisco (MRO planner, procurement process owner) and Mark (supply chain manager, inventory/warehouse owner). However, no quantified business case has been articulated — no stated cost of downtime, no procurement cycle-time reduction target, no working-capital savings goal, and no ROI model. The next move is to co-develop a quantified value case (procurement cycle-time savings, downtime-cost avoidance, working-capital reduction via consignment) with Francisco and the procurement team, anchored to their assessment data.

champion.narrative: Francisco Güereca is the developing champion, with strong process ownership and advocacy, but limited power and cooling engagement. Francisco (IT Manager/MRO Planner) completed a full procurement process assessment before the Zycus engagement, identified clear improvement opportunities (automated PO placement, consignment, supplier evaluation, eCatalogs), and brought Mark Patton (his manager, Director of Operations/Supply Chain) onto the 13 May 2026 call to advance the conversation. On that call, Francisco actively validated Zycus' fit ('your tool seems to be a better fit... he and I both feel the same on it, that there's a great opportunity to make better'), requested demo materials and look-and-feel examples to review with the procurement team, and committed to 'mull it over a little bit, then we can get back to you'. However, Francisco is an influencer (IT Manager/MRO Planner) with no commercial authority; he explicitly stated on 13 May 2026 that procurement leadership must be involved ('we would definitely have to get our procurement team involved in something like this if we were to go forward'). Mark Patton (Francisco's manager, Director of Operations/Supply Chain) is similarly an influencer, not a decision-maker, and noted 'you're not talking to anyone in our procurement team'. Neither Francisco nor Mark has access to the Economic Buyer or procurement leadership, and neither has driven multi-threading or introduced Zycus to the power roles. After receiving Bailey's follow-up materials (demo videos, slide decks) post-13 May call, Francisco and Mark have gone silent for 43 days — no reply, no procurement introduction, no confirmation of RFP scope or timeline. The champion is cooling. The move is to re-engage Francisco with a light, value-add check-in (a relevant proof point, e.g. a steel/manufacturing customer case study showing procurement cycle-time and working-capital savings), confirm whether he reviewed the materials with procurement, and ask for a joint call with the procurement leader to advance the deal.

sources: Competitors__c: empty
Avoma call 6 May 2026: Bailey Erazo asked 'do you guys use SAP Ariba by chance?' Mark Patton replied 'No. We are not using Ariba'
Avoma call 13 May 2026: Francisco Güereca stated 'more than 95% are doing manually. They place that PR into a PO manually' (implying no procurement automation tool in place)
Next_Step__c (~2 Jun 2026): 'Axis Pipe prospects: Confirm whether they want one combined RFP or separate RFPs' (no RFP issued, no competitors named)
No competitor mentioned in any call, Next_Step, or Salesforce field
competition.narrative: No competitor has been named or surfaced in any field, call, Next_Step, or task. The Competitors__c field is empty. Neither discovery call (6 May or 13 May 2026) mentioned another vendor, an RFP shortlist, or an incumbent procurement system being replaced. On 6 May 2026, Mark Patton stated that Axis Pipe uses SAP on-premise ERP for inventory and MRP-driven PR generation, and Bailey asked 'do you guys use SAP Ariba by chance?' to which Mark replied 'No. We are not using Ariba'. This rules out SAP Ariba as an incumbent. On 13 May 2026, Francisco Güereca noted that over 95% of PRs are manually converted to POs in SAP, implying no procurement automation tool is in place today. The implicit competitor is the status quo: manual procurement processes in SAP with no automation layer. However, the lack of any named peer vendor (Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua) or any mention of a competitive evaluation process suggests either (1) this is a sole-source or early-stage exploration, not yet a bake-off, or (2) the buyer has not disclosed the competitive landscape. The Next_Step__c (set ~2 Jun 2026) indicates the buyer is expected to confirm 'whether they want one combined RFP or separate RFPs', which implies a formal RFP may introduce competition, but no RFP has been issued and no competitor has been mentioned. The wedge is Zycus' procurement automation (iSource, iContract, iSupplier) with SAP integration, supplier performance/risk management, and eCatalog — addressing the specific pain Francisco and Mark articulated (manual PR-to-PO, zero tracking on critical items, no supplier accountability) — but this wedge is untested against any rival. The move is to ask Francisco and Mark directly whether they are evaluating other procurement platforms, whether an RFP will go out, and who else is in the frame, so we can position Zycus' differentiation (AI-powered, organically-built, 1,200+ SAP touchpoints, supplier portal, real-time tracking) early.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed, operationally specific, and articulated by the pain owners with urgency. On 13 May 2026, Francisco Güereca (MRO Planner, procurement process owner) stated that over 95% of purchase requisitions are manually converted from PR to PO in SAP, with procurement manually seeking quotes from 3 vendors per item — a time-intensive, error-prone process. Mark Patton (Director of Operations/Supply Chain Manager, warehouse and inventory owner) stated on 13 May 2026 that Axis Pipe has 'no good system in place to provide feedback on getting statuses on these items' (critical parts at zero stock), leading to 'potential line stopping because the lack of inventory for those critical items'. Francisco confirmed on the same call: 'we are on a potential land stopping because the lack of inventory in the warehouse for critical items... that is one of the most daily basis strollings that we have right now'. Francisco also noted that suppliers are chronically late and there is no contractual accountability ('any compensation from the vendor to us for all the delays'), and that Axis Pipe must manually run reports to estimate lead times because the system does not track them. Mark noted on 13 May 2026 that critical items hit zero stock and 'we struggle with getting updated lead times', forcing the team to manually calculate lead times from historical data. The pain is operational (manual, slow, error-prone procurement), financial (working capital tied up in inventory; downtime risk from stockouts), and strategic (inability to negotiate effectively or hold suppliers accountable). The pain owners are Francisco (procurement process/MRO planning) and Mark (supply chain/warehouse operations).

Avoma call 13 May 2026: Bailey Erazo stated 'we have about 12 to 1,400 integration touch points. So any information that you want to live in Zycus or SAP, both of it will flow bilaterally'
No discussion of MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, or procurement approval steps on either call
Current_Contract_Expiration__c: not populated in Salesforce
paper_process.narrative: Paper process (contracting, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec, implementation scoping) is entirely unknown. No discussion of MSA, SOW, Order Form, security review, or IT/InfoSec signoff appeared on either discovery call (6 May or 13 May 2026). No Current_Contract_Expiration__c field value exists to create a forcing function. Francisco Güereca is the IT Manager, suggesting IT will be involved in SAP integration scoping and possibly security review, but no formal IT approval or InfoSec process has been mentioned. On 13 May 2026, Francisco noted that Axis Pipe uses on-premise SAP ERP (not yet S/4HANA) with plans to migrate to cloud, and Bailey confirmed Zycus has ~1,200-1,400 SAP integration touchpoints with real-time bilateral sync, but no integration timeline, technical validation step, or IT gating process was discussed. The move is to ask Mark, Francisco, and the procurement team (once engaged) about the contracting and legal process: does a six-figure software purchase require legal review, CFO signoff, or a formal vendor onboarding/security questionnaire; and does the SAP integration require IT architecture review or a separate SOW.

economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is not identified. The two discovery calls (6 May and 13 May 2026) involved Mark Patton (Director of Operations/Supply Chain Manager) and Francisco Güereca (IT Manager/MRO Planner), both influencers with deep operational and process ownership but no commercial or budget authority. On 13 May 2026, Mark explicitly stated 'you're not talking to anyone in our procurement team... we would definitely have to get our procurement team involved in something like this if we were to go forward', signaling that procurement leadership (not yet engaged) likely controls the commercial decision. No CFO, VP Procurement, or executive sponsor has appeared on any call or in any contact role. The deal is single-threaded below the power line. The immediate move is to ask Mark and Francisco for an introduction to the procurement leader (VP or Director of Procurement) and to the budget authority (CFO or SVP Operations) who will approve a six-figure S2P investment, and to schedule a joint session with procurement leadership present.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially understood but not formally mapped. The call sequence (6 May: initial discovery with Mark Patton; 13 May: deeper session with Mark + Francisco Güereca, the MRO planner who completed a full procurement assessment) shows that Mark is the entry point and Francisco is the process/pain owner, but both are influencers, not decision-makers. On 13 May 2026, Mark stated 'you're not talking to anyone in our procurement team... we would definitely have to get our procurement team involved', indicating that procurement leadership (not yet engaged) will drive the evaluation and approval. Francisco said he would 'review with the procurement team' and 'look over this and kinda let us mull it over a little bit, then we can get back to you', then 'we would definitely have to get our procurement team involved in something like this if we were to go forward'. This implies a two-stage process: (1) Mark and Francisco vet the solution internally, then (2) procurement leadership takes over the formal evaluation. The Next_Step__c (set ~2 Jun 2026) records that Axis Pipe is expected to confirm the evaluation path (one combined RFP for S2P+SRM or separate RFPs) and share timeline, stakeholders, and requirements, but this has not been delivered after 43 days. No approval chain, budget cycle, or decision timeline has been disclosed. The move is to re-engage Mark and Francisco, confirm whether they have reviewed the materials with procurement, map the procurement leadership (title, role), and ask for a joint call to understand the approval process, budget authority, and timeline.

Next_Step__c (~2 Jun 2026): 'Axis Pipe prospects: Confirm whether they want one combined RFP or separate RFPs, share high-level requirements, timeline, stakeholders, and SAP integration expectations' (buyer has not delivered after 43 days)
decision_criteria.narrative: Partial decision criteria have emerged from the discovery calls but are not formally documented or validated by the buying committee. On 13 May 2026, Francisco Güereca and Mark Patton articulated operational priorities: (1) automated PR-to-PO conversion with supplier evaluation/multi-quote capability, (2) real-time PO tracking and delivery status updates to prevent critical-item stockouts and line stoppages, (3) supplier performance management (delivery times, quality) and contractual accountability (compensation for delays), (4) working-capital reduction via consignment and negotiated agreements, (5) SAP integration (on-premise SAP ERP, planned future migration to S/4HANA), and (6) eCatalog capability for procurement efficiency and spend analysis. Francisco specifically requested demo videos and look-and-feel materials to share with the procurement team (13 May 2026), indicating that procurement will apply their own evaluation lens. However, no formal RFP, scoring rubric, or buying-committee criteria have been shared. The Next_Step__c (set ~2 Jun 2026) records that Axis Pipe prospects are expected to 'confirm whether they want one combined RFP or separate RFPs, share high-level requirements, timeline, stakeholders, and SAP integration expectations', but this has not been delivered after 43 days. The move is to re-engage Mark and Francisco, confirm the evaluation process (RFP vs informal, combined S2P+SRM scope vs phased), and co-author the formal requirements with procurement leadership.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS field data (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c all null). From call content, AI appetite is not explicitly stated but operational priorities (automation, supplier evaluation, real-time tracking, eCatalogs) suggest openness to AI-powered procurement intelligence if tied to ROI. Francisco Güereca's procurement assessment and interest in eCatalogs and supplier evaluation align with AI-driven spend analysis and supplier risk management (Merlin capabilities). However, AI was not a stated requirement on either discovery call (6 May or 13 May 2026), and no AI-specific question or pain point was raised. Tier: AI Curious (open to AI if it solves the operational pain, but not actively seeking AI as a requirement).

items.change: Initial discovery call with Mark Patton (Director of Operations/Supply Chain Manager): established pain (highly manual processes, SAP on-premise ERP, MRP-driven PRs with manual PO conversion, interest in procurement automation and SAP integration)

Deeper discovery call with Mark Patton + Francisco Güereca (IT Manager/MRO Planner): Francisco presented findings from full procurement assessment, identified clear opportunities (automated PO placement, consignment, supplier evaluation, eCatalogs, real-time critical-item tracking). Bailey demo'd Zycus platform (iSource, iContract, iSupplier, Merlin). Francisco requested demo videos and look-and-feel materials to share with procurement team. Mark and Francisco committed to review materials and involve procurement leadership.

Next_Step__c updated: Bailey to send follow-up summarizing S2P + Supplier Risk Management scope and coordinate deeper discovery/demo; Axis Pipe to confirm RFP scope (combined or separate), requirements, timeline, stakeholders, SAP integration expectations
No buyer reply or activity since last contact; buyer-dependent action (confirm scope, timeline, stakeholders) unaddressed for 43 days. Engagement cooling.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 14 May 2026 (~6 weeks ago) following two strong discovery calls (6 May and 13 May 2026, both ~6-7 weeks ago). On 13 May 2026, Bailey demo'd the Zycus platform (iSource, iContract, iSupplier, Merlin) and Francisco Güereca requested demo videos and materials to review with the procurement team. Next_Step__c was updated ~2 Jun 2026 (24 days ago) recording Bailey's commitment to send a follow-up and Axis Pipe's commitment to confirm RFP scope, timeline, stakeholders, and SAP integration expectations. Last verified Salesforce activity: 14 May 2026 (43 days ago). No buyer reply or activity since. Engagement is cooling: buyer-dependent action (confirm scope, timeline, stakeholders) unaddressed for 43 days. Stage: Qualified for 43 days with no progression.

However, no reply in 43 days and buyer-dependent commitment (confirm RFP scope, timeline, stakeholders) undelivered. Champion is developing but at risk of fading.

items.sentiment: Positive. However, noted 'you're not talking to anyone in our procurement team... we would definitely have to get our procurement team involved', signaling he is not the decision-maker. No reply in 43 days.

items.detail: Economic Buyer and procurement leadership not identified or engaged. Only two influencers (Mark Patton, Francisco Güereca) mapped after two discovery calls. Mark stated on 13 May 2026: 'you're not talking to anyone in our procurement team... we would definitely have to get our procurement team involved'. Deal cannot advance without procurement leadership and budget authority engagement.

No timeline, decision date, or budget cycle disclosed. Next_Step__c (~2 Jun 2026) records that Axis Pipe is expected to confirm timeline and stakeholders, but this has not been delivered after 43 days. Close date 31 Dec 2026 (188 days away) is a placeholder with no buyer validation.

Champion cooling: Francisco Güereca (developing champion) has not replied in 43 days and has not delivered the buyer-dependent commitment (confirm RFP scope, timeline, stakeholders, SAP integration expectations). If Francisco loses interest or bandwidth, the deal fades. No fallback champion or multi-thread path established.

No budget, budget authority, or CFO approval process discussed. Amount $100,000 is a placeholder estimate, not a buyer-validated figure. No confirmation that a six-figure S2P investment is budgeted or approved for 2026.

SAP integration expectations and IT approval process not formalized. Francisco Güereca noted on 13 May 2026 that Axis Pipe uses on-premise SAP ERP (not yet S/4HANA) with plans to migrate to cloud, and Bailey confirmed Zycus has 1,200+ SAP touchpoints with real-time bilateral sync, but no integration timeline, technical validation step, IT gating process, or SAP architecture review was discussed. Next_Step__c (~2 Jun 2026) records that buyer is expected to share SAP integration expectations, but this has not been delivered.

Competitive risk via RFP: Next_Step__c (~2 Jun 2026) indicates buyer may issue an RFP ('Confirm whether they want one combined RFP or separate RFPs'), which could introduce peer vendors (Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua) not yet named. No RFP has been released and no competitor surfaced in 43 days, but the RFP path is unconfirmed and Zycus' sole-source position is fragile until the buyer commits to a direct evaluation or names Zycus as the preferred vendor.

items.trigger: Pain is confirmed and operationally specific (95%+ manual PR-to-PO conversion, line-stoppage risk from critical-item stockouts, chronic supplier delays, manual lead-time estimation per Francisco Güereca and Mark Patton on 13 May 2026), but not quantified in dollar or time-savings terms. No ROI model, procurement cycle-time reduction target, downtime-cost estimate, or working-capital savings goal articulated. Without a quantified business case, the deal lacks urgency and justification for a six-figure investment.
items.expected_effect: Create urgency and justify the investment by quantifying the cost of the status quo (manual procurement cycle time, line-stoppage downtime cost, working capital tied up in warehouse) and the ROI of Zycus (automated PR-to-PO, real-time tracking, supplier accountability, consignment). Position Merlin as the AI layer that amplifies the savings. A quantified business case (e.g. '20% procurement cycle-time reduction = $X savings; 50% reduction in line-stoppage events = $Y downtime-cost avoidance; 15% working-capital reduction via consignment = $Z freed up') is the deliverable the Economic Buyer and CFO need to approve the investment.

items.action: Confirm the evaluation path: ask Francisco and Mark whether Axis Pipe will issue a formal RFP (combined S2P + SRM or separate) or conduct a direct evaluation with Zycus, and if an RFP, ask for the requirements draft, timeline, and shortlist to position Zycus' differentiation early.
items.trigger: Next_Step__c (~2 Jun 2026) records that Axis Pipe is expected to 'confirm whether they want one combined RFP or separate RFPs, share high-level requirements, timeline, stakeholders, and SAP integration expectations'. This has not been delivered after 43 days. No RFP has been issued and no competitor has surfaced, but the evaluation path (RFP vs direct) is undefined, and an RFP could introduce peer vendors (Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua) not yet named.
items.expected_effect: Clarify the evaluation process (RFP vs direct, combined S2P+SRM scope vs phased) and timeline. If an RFP will be issued, co-author the requirements with Francisco and procurement leadership to ensure Zycus' strengths (AI-powered, organically-built, 1,200+ SAP touchpoints, supplier portal, real-time tracking, Merlin) are weighted in the scoring criteria. If a direct evaluation, lock in Zycus as the preferred vendor before competition enters. Remove uncertainty and position Zycus' differentiation early.

items.action: Position Merlin (AI-powered procurement intelligence) as the differentiator by tying it to Francisco's assessment priorities: predictive supplier risk, intelligent contract clause extraction, automated spend categorization, and negotiation-planning insights.
items.trigger: Merlin was introduced on the 13 May 2026 demo call but not yet anchored to a buyer pain or use case. Bailey emphasized 'AI powered' and 'organically grown' as differentiators, but Francisco and Mark did not ask follow-up questions about Merlin or AI-specific features. Products field includes 'Merlin', indicating it is in scope, but no AI-specific requirement, pain point, or decision criterion has been articulated by the buyer. AI positioning is under-positioned.
items.expected_effect: Elevate Zycus' AI differentiation (Merlin) from a feature to a strategic value driver by linking it to Francisco's assessment priorities (supplier evaluation, negotiation planning, working-capital optimization). Show how Merlin delivers predictive supplier risk (e.g. flag a supplier's financial distress before a critical delivery fails), intelligent contract clause extraction (e.g. auto-identify unfavorable payment terms or missing compensation-for-delay clauses), and automated spend categorization (e.g. surface negotiation opportunities in high-spend categories). Position Merlin as the AI layer that amplifies the ROI of iSource, iContract, and iSupplier, and makes Zycus the future-proof choice vs peer vendors.

items: Two substantive discovery calls (6 May and 13 May 2026) with strong pain validation and solution fit. Francisco Güereca (developing champion) validated Zycus fit and requested demo materials on 13 May 2026.
No buyer reply or activity in 43 days since LastActivityDate 14 May 2026. Buyer-dependent commitment (confirm RFP scope, timeline, stakeholders, SAP integration expectations) undelivered for 43 days.
Single-threaded: only two influencers (Mark Patton, Francisco Güereca) engaged. No EB, DM, or procurement leadership mapped or engaged.
Champion cooling: Francisco Güereca (developing champion) has not replied or delivered buyer-dependent commitment in 43 days.
confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling. Two strong discovery calls (6 May and 13 May 2026, ~6-7 weeks ago) established pain, solution fit, and champion advocacy (Francisco Güereca), but no buyer reply or activity in 43 days since LastActivityDate 14 May 2026. Buyer-dependent commitment (confirm RFP scope, timeline, stakeholders, SAP integration expectations per Next_Step__c ~2 Jun 2026) remains undelivered. Single-threaded below the power line (only two influencers, no EB/DM/procurement leadership engaged). Deal is alive but stalled.

north_star_verdict.math: 188 days to close (close date 31 Dec 2026). Created 14 May 2026 (~6 weeks ago). Stage: Qualified. Time in stage: ~43 days with no progression. Last verified activity: 14 May 2026 (43 days ago). Two discovery calls (6 May and 13 May, both ~6-7 weeks ago) established strong pain and solution fit, but the buyer-dependent action item (confirm RFP scope, timeline, stakeholders, SAP integration expectations) remains unaddressed for 43 days. No buyer reply to Bailey's follow-up, no multi-threading beyond two influencers (Mark Patton and Francisco Güereca), and no progression toward Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted. The deal is alive but stalled on a buyer-side commitment.
evidence: Two substantive discovery calls (6 May and 13 May 2026, ~6-7 weeks ago) established strong operational pain: 95%+ manual PR-to-PO conversion, zero real-time tracking on critical items causing line-stoppage risk, and chronic supplier delivery delays with no vendor accountability (Francisco Güereca, 13 May 2026).

Francisco completed a full procurement process assessment and identified clear opportunities: automated PO placement, consignment for critical parts to reduce working capital, supplier evaluation/negotiation, and eCatalogs (Francisco Güereca, 13 May 2026).

Zycus platform demo on 13 May 2026 covered iSource, iContract, iSupplier, Merlin and was well-received; Francisco requested demo videos and look-and-feel materials to review with procurement team (13 May 2026).

Next_Step__c (set ~2 Jun 2026, 24 days ago) records Bailey's commitment to send a follow-up summarizing Zycus' S2P + Supplier Risk Management scope and coordinate a deeper discovery/demo, AND Axis Pipe's commitment to confirm RFP scope (combined or separate), requirements, timeline, stakeholders, and SAP integration expectations.
No buyer reply or activity in 43 days since last contact (14 May 2026). The buyer-dependent action (confirm scope, timeline, stakeholders) remains unaddressed.

Single-threaded: only two influencers (Mark Patton, Director of Operations/Supply Chain Manager; Francisco Güereca, IT Manager) engaged. No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or procurement leadership mapped.
Stage velocity: Qualified for 43 days with no movement toward Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted despite strong initial fit.
north_star_verdict.headline: Strong discovery (6-13 May) established clear pain and solution fit with two influencers, but buyer silent 43 days on critical next steps: RFP scope, timeline, stakeholders, and SAP integration. No multi-threading to procurement leadership; stalled on buyer commitment.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal with strong pain validation and solution fit, but single-threaded below the power line and stalled on a buyer-side commitment for 43 days. The highest-leverage moves are: (1) re-engage the developing champion (Francisco Güereca) with a value-add touch to restart momentum, (2) secure an introduction to procurement leadership and the Economic Buyer to multi-thread above the power line, and (3) co-develop a quantified business case (procurement cycle-time savings, downtime-cost avoidance, working-capital reduction) to create urgency and justify the investment.

competitive_position.summary: No named competitor. The only confirmed alternative is the status quo: manual procurement processes in SAP with no automation layer. SAP Ariba was ruled out on the 6 May 2026 call (Mark Patton: 'We are not using Ariba'). No other peer vendor (Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua) or RFP shortlist has been mentioned. The Next_Step__c (~2 Jun 2026) indicates the buyer may issue an RFP, which could introduce competition, but no RFP has been released and no competitor has surfaced in 43 days. The current threat is inaction / status quo: if Francisco and Mark do not engage procurement leadership and the deal stalls, the manual process continues. Zycus is positioned as the only known vendor in the frame, but the lack of procurement-leadership engagement and the 43-day silence suggest the deal may fade before competition even appears.

competitors.name: Status quo (manual procurement in SAP, no automation)
competitors.quote: More than 95% are doing manually. They place that PR into a PO manually, looking for a better price, looking for maybe 3 vendors. (Francisco Güereca, 13 May 2026)
competitors.how_we_win: Automate the 95% manual PR-to-PO conversion with iSource (multi-vendor quoting, automated PO placement, supplier evaluation). Deliver real-time critical-item tracking and supplier performance management (iSupplier) to prevent line stoppages. Enable consignment and negotiation planning to reduce working capital. Provide seamless SAP integration (1,200+ touchpoints, bilateral sync) and eCatalogs. Position Merlin as the AI layer for procurement intelligence. Quantify the savings (procurement cycle-time, downtime-cost avoidance, working-capital reduction) and make the status quo expensive.

items.quote: Maybe a good idea just to have a better feeling of what you are explaining is to if you can share, not today, but maybe in the future, if you can share with us some screens about the look and feel... to have a better understanding of your system. (Francisco Güereca, 13 May 2026). Bailey replied: 'We have some short demo videos. They're like 5 minutes long as well as a couple slide decks that I can send over... I'll shoot that information over' (13 May 2026).
items.requirement: Share demo videos, slide decks, and look-and-feel materials showing Zycus system interface and workflows

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning (Merlin) was introduced on the 13 May 2026 demo call but not yet anchored to a buyer pain or use case. Bailey demo'd the full Zycus platform including Merlin (AI-powered procurement intelligence) and emphasized 'AI powered' and 'organically grown' as differentiators, but Francisco and Mark did not ask follow-up questions about Merlin or AI-specific features. The Products field includes 'Merlin', indicating it is in scope, but no AI-specific requirement, pain point, or decision criterion has been articulated by the buyer. The deal is not AI-led; procurement automation (iSource, iContract, iSupplier) is the primary value driver. Move: on re-engagement, position Merlin as the AI layer that amplifies procurement savings (e.g. predictive supplier risk, intelligent contract clause extraction, automated spend categorization) and tie it to Francisco's assessment priorities (supplier evaluation, negotiation planning, working-capital optimization).

items.quote: More than 95% are doing manually. They place that PR into a PO manually, looking for a better price, looking for maybe 3 vendors. (Francisco Güereca, 13 May 2026). Bailey demo'd iSource and eProcurement modules showing automated PO placement, sourcing events, and supplier scoring (13 May 2026).
items.criterion: Automated PR-to-PO conversion with multi-vendor quoting and supplier evaluation

items.quote: We do not have a good system in place to provide feedback on getting statuses on these items... we struggle with getting updates on it... that creates... potential line stopping because the lack of inventory for those critical items. (Mark Patton, 13 May 2026). Bailey demo'd eInvoicing and supplier portal showing real-time PO tracking and historical delivery-time analysis (13 May 2026).
items.criterion: Real-time PO tracking and delivery status updates to prevent critical-item stockouts and line stoppages

items.quote: We need to implement or improve the PO confirmation and the PO tracking... to have a commitment from our vendor to us... any compensation from the vendor to us for all the delays. (Francisco Güereca, 13 May 2026). Bailey demo'd iSupplier (supplier performance management, delivery-time tracking, quality-of-goods tracking) and iContract (contract repository, clause management, utilization tracking) (13 May 2026).
items.criterion: Supplier performance management and contractual accountability (compensation for delays)

items.quote: Consignment to improve the consignment... just in time delivery, zero cost value in our inventory... to reduce our working capital in the warehouse. (Francisco Güereca, 13 May 2026). Bailey demo'd iContract and iSource for agreement management and negotiation planning (13 May 2026).
items.criterion: Working-capital reduction via consignment and negotiated agreements

items.quote: We're not the s4 HANA... we're in our own system. We're not in the cloud yet, but we are working our way to go to that. (Francisco Güereca, 13 May 2026). Bailey stated 'we have about 12 to 1,400 integration touch points. So any information that you want to live in Zycus or SAP, both of it will flow bilaterally... it will update in real time on each other software' (13 May 2026).
items.criterion: SAP integration (on-premise SAP ERP, planned future S/4HANA migration)

items.quote: Thinking about the future, what about looking about the e catalogs... planning that kind of situation for the future here in access. (Francisco Güereca, 13 May 2026). Bailey demo'd supplier portal and eCatalog functionality: 'whenever a supplier updates their catalog, you guys have the newest catalog for that specific supplier. So whenever an end user goes in to create a purchase requisition, all the pricing details or actual item numbers, part numbers are all gonna match' (13 May 2026).
items.criterion: eCatalog capability for procurement efficiency and spend analysis

items.quote: Next_Step__c (~2 Jun 2026): 'Axis Pipe prospects: Confirm whether they want one combined RFP or separate RFPs, share high-level requirements, timeline, stakeholders, and SAP integration expectations.' Buyer has not delivered this after 43 days. Scope, timeline, and stakeholders remain undefined.
items.criterion: Scope confirmation: combined S2P + Supplier Risk Management vs separate RFPs; requirements, timeline, stakeholders, SAP integration expectations

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on operational requirements (automated PR-to-PO, real-time tracking, supplier performance/risk, SAP integration, eCatalogs) as articulated by Francisco Güereca and Mark Patton on 13 May 2026. Zycus' iSource, iContract, iSupplier, and Merlin modules directly address the pain. However, fit is unvalidated by procurement leadership (not yet engaged) and no formal requirements, scoring criteria, or RFP have been shared. The buyer's expectations for combined S2P + Supplier Risk Management scope, timeline, stakeholders, and SAP integration details remain unconfirmed after 43 days.

hard.ais_why: Prospect showed clear interest in a unified S2P + Supplier Risk platform, but there was no explicit discussion around AI-specific capabilities, AI agents, automation intelligence, or GenAI use cases. AI interest is implied only through the need for risk monitoring, alerts, supplier insights, and process efficiency.
hard.next_step:
• Bailey: Send follow-up to Axis Pipe summarizing Zycus’ ability to support a combined S2P + Supplier Risk Management scope, and coordinate a deeper discovery / tailored demo.
  • Axis Pipe prospects: Confirm whether they want one combined RFP or separate RFPs, share high-level requirements, timeline, stakeholders, and SAP integration expectations.

Francisco Güereca noted on 13 May 2026 that Axis Pipe uses on-premise SAP ERP (not yet S/4HANA) with plans to migrate to cloud, and Bailey confirmed Zycus has 1,200+ SAP touchpoints with real-time bilateral sync, but no integration timeline, technical validation step, IT gating process, or SAP architecture review was discussed. Next_Step__c (~2 Jun 2026) records that buyer is expected to share SAP integration expectations, but this has not been delivered.

value.detail: Competitive risk via RFP: Next_Step__c (~2 Jun 2026) indicates buyer may issue an RFP ('Confirm whether they want one combined RFP or separate RFPs'), which could introduce peer vendors (Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua) not yet named. No RFP has been released and no competitor surfaced in 43 days, but the RFP path is unconfirmed and Zycus' sole-source position is fragile until the buyer commits to a direct evaluation or names Zycus as the preferred vendor.

packets.subject: Competitive risk via RFP: Next_Step__c (~2 Jun 2026) indicates buyer may issue an RFP ('Confirm whether they want one combined RFP or separate RFPs'), which could introduce peer vendors (Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua) not yet named. value.flag: Single-threaded below the power line: Only two influencers engaged (Mark Patton, Director of Operations/Supply Chain; Francisco Güereca, IT Manager/MRO Planner). No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or procurement leadership identified or engaged after two discovery calls (6 May and 13 May 2026). Mark stated on 13 May 2026: 'you're not talking to anyone in our procurement team... we would definitely have to get our procurement team involved'. The deal cannot advance to the relevant stakeholder or Shortlisted without procurement leadership and budget authority mapped and engaged. Move: ask Francisco and Mark for an introduction to the VP or Director of Procurement and the CFO/budget authority, and schedule a joint call.

Move: ask Francisco and Mark for an introduction to the VP or Director of Procurement and the CFO/budget authority, and schedule a joint call.

value.flag: Champion cooling after 43-day silence: Francisco Güereca is the developing champion (completed full procurement assessment, validated Zycus fit, requested demo materials on 13 May 2026), but has not replied in 43 days and has not delivered the buyer-dependent commitment (confirm RFP scope, timeline, stakeholders, SAP integration expectations per Next_Step__c ~2 Jun 2026). The deal is at risk of fading if Francisco loses interest or bandwidth. Move: re-engage Francisco with a light, value-add check-in (e.g. a steel/manufacturing customer case study showing procurement automation ROI, or a relevant Merlin/AI insight on supplier risk), confirm whether he reviewed the materials with procurement, and ask for a joint call with procurement leadership to advance the deal.

packets.subject: Champion cooling after 43-day silence: Francisco Güereca is the developing champion (completed full procurement assessment, validated Zycus fit, requested demo materials on 13 May 2026), but has not replied in 43 days and has not delivered the buyer-dependent commitment (confirm RFP scope, timeline, stakeholders, SAP integration expectations per Next_Step__c ~2 Jun 2026). The deal is at risk of fading if Francisco loses interest or bandwidth. Move: re-engage Francisco with a light, value-add check-in (e.g. a steel/manufacturing customer case study showing procurement automation ROI, or a relevant Merlin/AI insight on supplier risk), confirm whether he reviewed the materials with procurement, and ask for a joint call with procurement leadership to advance the deal.

value.flag: No quantified business case: Pain is confirmed (95%+ manual PR-to-PO conversion, line-stoppage risk from critical-item stockouts, chronic supplier delays, manual lead-time estimation) but not quantified in dollar or time-savings terms. Without a quantified ROI model (procurement cycle-time reduction, downtime-cost avoidance, working-capital savings via consignment), the deal lacks urgency and justification for a six-figure S2P investment. Move: co-develop a quantified value case with Francisco, Mark, and the procurement team, anchored to Francisco's procurement assessment data (current cycle times, stockout frequency/cost, working capital in warehouse, supplier delay frequency). Position Merlin as the AI layer that amplifies the savings.

gaps: Economic Buyer and budget authority not identified after two discovery calls (6 May and 13 May 2026). Mark Patton stated on 13 May 2026: 'you're not talking to anyone in our procurement team'.
Decision Maker and procurement leadership not engaged. No VP or Director of Procurement mapped.
Quantified business case (ROI, procurement cycle-time savings, downtime-cost avoidance, working-capital reduction) not articulated on calls or in fields.
RFP scope, timeline, stakeholders, and SAP integration expectations not confirmed by buyer after 43 days (per Next_Step__c ~2 Jun 2026).
Paper process (contracting, legal review, InfoSec, IT approval for SAP integration) not discussed on calls.

Multi-threading: only 2 buyer-side contact roles (Mark Patton, Francisco Güereca) on the opportunity, below the multi-thread bar. No EB, DM, Executive Sponsor, or procurement leadership contact roles.
